LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BARBARA BUCHMAN AND INTERNATIONAL LYME AND ASSOCIATED DISEASES SOCIETY'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND DEFAMATION IN PLAINTIFF RAPHAEL STRICKER'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THEIR FIRST PRONG BURDEN THAT THE DECLARATORY RELIEF AND DEFAMATION CLAIMS ARISE FROM ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE, DR. STRICKER HAS SHOWN THAT THERE IS MINIMAL MERIT TO BOTH OF THE CLAIMS. DR. STRICKER'S DECLARATION PRESENTS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT, PER THE REVERSE SUMMARY JUDGMENT PROCEDURE THAT GOVERNS THE SECOND PRONG, THERE IS A TRIABLE DISPUTE WHETHER DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH MALICE TO DEFEAT THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE OF THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE. DR. STRICKER'S DECLARATION SHOWS THAT DEFENDANTS WERE AWARE THAT THE ARTICLE THAT HE SOUGHT TO HAVE PUBLISHED BY DOVE PRESS AND CHRONIC DISEASE INTERNATIONAL WAS HIS ORIGINAL UNPLAGIARIZED WORK YET THEY KNOWINGLY STATED OTHERWISE AND TOOK ACTIONS TO PREVENT THE PUBLICATION OF THE ARTICLE AS WELL AS REMOVED DR. STRICKER FROM THE PARIS CONFERENCE. THIS CONDUCT, IF BELIEVED TO HAVE OCCURRED, SHOWS THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED IN RECKLESS DISREGARD OF DR. STRIKER'S RIGHTS AND ALSO TENDS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS WERE MOTIVATED BY ILL WILL TOWARDS DR. STRICKER. DR. STRICKER'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED BECAUSE THIS MOTION IS NEITHER FRIVOLOUS NOR SOLELY INTENDED TO CAUSE DELAY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Under Anti-Slapp Statute
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Barbara Buchman and International Lyme and Associated Diseases Society's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the second and third causes of action for declaratory relief and defamation in plaintiff Raphael Stricker's complaint is denied. Assuming without deciding that defendants satisfied their first prong burden that the declaratory relief and defamation claims arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute, Dr. Stricker has shown that there is minimal merit to both of the claims. Dr. Stricker's declaration presents sufficient evidence to show that, per the reverse summary judgment procedure that governs the second prong, there is a triable dispute whether defendants acted with malice to defeat their affirmative defense of the common interest privilege. Dr. Stricker's declaration shows that defendants were aware that the article that he sought to have published by Dove Press and Chronic Disease International was his original unplagiarized work yet they knowingly stated otherwise and took actions to prevent the publication of the article as well as removed Dr. Stricker from the Paris conference. This conduct, if believed to have occurred, shows that defendants acted in reckless disregard of Dr. Striker's rights and also tends to show that defendants' actions were motivated by ill will towards Dr. Stricker. Dr. Stricker's request for attorney's fees is denied because this motion is neither frivolous nor solely intended to cause delay. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
